t_ch: 来到第三个孔位…相信你一定能找到合适的人选。
t_en: It's time for the third slot... I just know you'll think of a suitable candidate for this.

t_ch: 丹恒…
t_en: Dan Heng...

t_ch: 丹恒！当然了，列车组的智库管理员兼你的好伙伴怎么能错过这种好事？
t_en: Dan Heng! Of course, the data bank administrator of the Astral Express and your good friend. How could he miss out on this?

t_ch: 这样就可以了吧？
t_en: Is this alright?

t_ch: 卡芙卡…
t_en: Kafka...

t_ch: 卡芙卡，你生命中既熟悉又陌生的那个女人…也许她会答应你的请求。
t_en: Kafka, the lady in your life that you are both familiar with and yet don't know at all... Perhaps she will agree to your invitation.

t_ch: 「对不起，我正在执行任务，」她的声音依旧温柔，「但你一定要玩得开心，好吗？」噢…真是太可惜了。
t_en: "Sorry, I'm on a mission right now." Her voice is as soft and gentle as ever. "But I hope you have a great time!" Ah... That's a real shame.

t_ch: 黄泉…
t_en: Acheron...

t_ch: 那位巡海游侠…或者别的什么隐世高手。不知道她现在正身在何方？
t_en: The Galaxy Ranger... Or secret master or whatever. I wonder where she is right now?

t_ch: 用镜头记录眼前的一瞬…真是新奇的体验。
t_en: Using a lens to record a moment before your very eyes... What a novel experience!

t_ch: 寒鸦…
t_en: Hanya...

t_ch: 真不知道这种既新奇又复古的文旅项目能否重燃判官大人对生活的热爱。
t_en: I wonder if this kind of novel-yet-retro tourist activity would be able to reignite the judge's passion for life?

t_ch: 啊，能在这儿站一会什么也不做真是太好了，而且还是离因果殿这么远的地方，一想到要回去继续工作我就忍不住希望这一刻能永远永远永远持续下去……
t_en: It's so good to stand here and do nothing. And this is so far away from the Hall of Karma. The mere thought of having to return to mountains of criminal reports makes me wish I could stand here for ever and ever and ever...

t_ch: …这段台词真的很眼熟。也许是错觉吧。
t_en: ...That script seemed a little familiar. Maybe I'm imagining it.

t_ch: 黑天鹅…
t_en: Black Swan...

t_ch: 神秘优雅的黑天鹅…没准她现在就在这里，只是没显形呢。
t_en: The mysterious and elegant Black Swan... She might even already be here. She just hasn't shown herself.

t_ch: 我还在猜你什么时候会想起我呢…谢谢你，亲爱的。
t_en: I was wondering when you would think of me... Thank you, dear.

t_ch: …她果然真就在这里。一语成谶。
t_en: ...She really was already here. Seriously, speak of the Devil...

t_ch: 最后一个孔位。漫长的选择题就要结束了，加油！
t_en: This is the final slot. The lengthy selection process is almost over. Keep going!

t_ch: 三月七…
t_en: March 7th...

t_ch: 还犹豫什么？这种场合当然得有三月七！
t_en: What are you waiting for? Of course March 7th needs to be here for something like this!

t_ch: 就是说…能不能给咱换个好看点的立牌？
t_en: I mean... Can we swap this for a prettier photo board?

t_ch: 桂乃芬…
t_en: Guinaifen...

t_ch: 相信这位热情似火的仙舟女孩一定不想错过这么热闹的场合。
t_en: That Xianzhou girl with her fiery passion would not want to miss out on a lively scene like this!

t_ch: 呀，这么多家人都来啦？让小桂子我好好拗个造型……
t_en: Ah, all the fam are here? Let me pull a cute pose...

t_ch: 阮•梅…
t_en: Ruan Mei...

t_ch: 天才俱乐部的阮•梅女士…记得你只和她有过一面之缘。
t_en: Madam Ruan Mei of the Genius Society... You've only met her once!

t_ch: 电话里传来「您呼叫的用户不在服务区」的声音。不知道这位天才女士是不是又找到了一处适合研究的世外桃源。
t_en: "The number you have called is currently out of the service area." I wonder if this genius lady has stumbled across another off-world research paradise...

t_ch: 雪衣…
t_en: Xueyi...

t_ch: 冰冷的仙舟机巧少女会接到你的电话吗？
t_en: Do you think the cold Xianzhou ingenium girl will accept your call?

t_ch: 电话始终无人接听。看来她那副棺材隔音真的很好。
t_en: The call was never answered. Her coffin must be really well sound-proofed!

t_ch: 终于！想要凑齐这么多人可真不容易。是时候为大家合影了！
t_en: Finally! Getting this many people together is no mean feat. It's time to take a group picture!

t_ch: 跟我一起说——
t_en: Everyone, together...

t_ch: ——茄子！
t_en: ...Cheese!

t_ch: 尽管面对镜头微笑对一些人来说真的很难，但你还是收获了一张绝好的合影。
t_en: Though staring into a lens and smiling is very difficult for some people, you still managed to get a wonderful group picture.

t_ch: （大家都回去了…和朋友们偶尔这样聚一聚也不错嘛。）
t_en: (Everyone has gone back... It's nice to get together with friends like this now and then, isn't it?)

t_ch: （大家都回去了…列车组难得能在这种场合聚在一起，真好。）
t_en: (Everyone has gone back... It's not often that the Express Crew gets to get together like this, that was lovely.)

t_ch: 一幅仓鼠球主题的挖洞合影立牌。你可以呼朋唤友，让他们把自己美貌的脸庞摆在合适的位置，与卡通立牌产生奇妙的化学反应，然后用相机记录下来。
t_en: A group photo board with face slots, featuring a Hamster Ball theme. You can call your friends and get them to place their beautiful faces in the appropriate slots to facilitate wondrous chemical reactions with the cartoon board. Then use your camera to record it!

t_ch: 机会难得，你想让你的朋友们试试看吗？
t_en: As you're here already, do you want to call your friends to give it a try?

t_ch: 当然！让我看看…
t_en: Of course! Let me think...

t_ch: 第一个孔位！满足这个身高要求的人可不算多，仔细想想吧。
t_en: The first slot! There aren't many people that would meet the height requirements for this. Have a good think...

t_ch: 阿兰…
t_en: Arlan...

t_ch: 不知道艾丝妲的空间站和你哪个更重要……
t_en: I wonder if you are more important than Asta's space station...?

t_ch: 我来了，{NICKNAME}。这是要我…合影么？
t_en: I'm here, {NICKNAME}. You want... to take a picture together?

t_ch: 恭喜你，看来确实是你更重要。
t_en: Congratulations! It seems you are more important.

t_ch: 彦卿…
t_en: Yanqing...

t_ch: 少年任侠彦卿…你身为「老师」，他应该没有理由拒绝你的邀请。
t_en: The young and chivalrous Yanqing... As his teacher, he should have no reason to refuse your invitation.

t_ch: 又见面了，老师！别担心，我这次远行有将军特许…只要晚上训练前能回到罗浮便是。
t_en: We meet again, teacher! Fear not, I have special permission from the general for this trip... I just have to be back to the Luofu in time for evening training.

t_ch: 我的朋友很少…
t_en: I don't have many friends...

t_ch: 噢，真可惜…那你快去多交点朋友吧。
t_en: Ah, that's a shame... Better go and make some more friends, then!

t_ch: 接下来是第二个孔位，也是最后一个孔位。满足这个条件的人倒是不少，你可以仔细想想。
t_en: Next, the second and final slot... There are a lot more people that meet the requirements for this one, so think carefully.

t_ch: 姬子…
t_en: Himeko...

t_ch: 姬子小姐！当然了，列车组的领航员怎么能错过这种好事？
t_en: Miss Himeko! Of course, how could the Express Crew navigator miss out on a treat like this?

t_ch: 呵呵，原来是想要合影…没问题。
t_en: Hehe, so you wanted to take a group picture... No problem.

t_ch: 卡芙卡…
t_en: Kafka...

t_ch: 卡芙卡，你生命中既熟悉又陌生的那个女人…也许她会答应你的请求。
t_en: Kafka, the lady in your life that you are both familiar with and yet don't know at all... Perhaps she will agree to your invitation.

t_ch: 「对不起，我正在执行任务，」她的声音依旧温柔，「但你一定要玩得开心，好吗？」噢…真是太可惜了。
t_en: "Sorry, I'm on a mission right now." Her voice is as soft and gentle as ever. "But I hope you have a great time!" Ah... That's a real shame.

t_ch: 黄泉…
t_en: Acheron...

t_ch: 那位巡海游侠…或者别的什么隐世高手。不知道她现在正身在何方？
t_en: The Galaxy Ranger... Or secret master or whatever. I wonder where she is right now?

t_ch: 用镜头记录眼前的一瞬…真是新奇的体验。
t_en: Using a lens to record a moment before your very eyes... What a novel experience!

t_ch: 寒鸦…
t_en: Hanya...

t_ch: 真不知道这种既新奇又复古的文旅项目能否重燃判官大人对生活的热爱。
t_en: I wonder if this kind of novel-yet-retro tourist activity would be able to reignite the judge's passion for life?

t_ch: 啊，能在这儿站一会什么也不做真是太好了，而且还是离因果殿这么远的地方，一想到要回去继续工作我就忍不住希望这一刻能永远永远永远持续下去……
t_en: It's so good to stand here and do nothing. And this is so far away from the Hall of Karma. The mere thought of having to return to mountains of criminal reports makes me wish I could stand here for ever and ever and ever...

t_ch: …这段台词真的很眼熟。也许是错觉吧。
t_en: ...That script seemed a little familiar. Maybe I'm imagining it.

t_ch: 黑天鹅…
t_en: Black Swan...

t_ch: 神秘优雅的黑天鹅…没准她现在就在这里，只是没显形呢。
t_en: The mysterious and elegant Black Swan... She might even already be here. She just hasn't shown herself.

t_ch: 我还在猜你什么时候会想起我呢…谢谢你，亲爱的。
t_en: I was wondering when you would think of me... Thank you, dear.

t_ch: …她果然真就在这里。一语成谶。
t_en: ...She really was already here. Seriously, speak of the Devil...

t_ch: 终于！想要凑齐这么多人可真不容易。是时候为大家合影了！
t_en: Finally! Getting this many people together is no mean feat. It's time to take a group picture!

t_ch: 跟我一起说——
t_en: Everyone, together...

t_ch: ——茄子！
t_en: ...Cheese!

t_ch: 尽管面对镜头微笑对一些人来说真的很难，但你还是收获了一张绝好的合影。
t_en: Though staring into a lens and smiling is very difficult for some people, you still managed to get a wonderful group picture.

t_ch: （大家都回去了…和朋友们偶尔这样聚一聚也不错嘛。）
t_en: (Everyone has gone back... It's nice to get together with friends like this now and then, isn't it?)

t_ch: 这位{F#女士}{M#先生}，请留步，容我为您介绍一下我的信仰——伟大的桶王，庇护万千桶的桶之神。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, please stay and allow me to introduce you to my faith — the mighty King of Cans, the God of Cans who shelters thousands of cans.

t_ch: 祂倾倒在寰宇间，九千只眼睛在三万个桶与六万只手臂上闪烁。其伟力令桶不再锈蚀，其注视让无机物生意盎然。
t_en: Admired by the universe, THEIR nine thousand eyes blink on the thirty thousand cans and sixty thousand arms. THEIR greatness prevents the cans from rusting, and THEIR gaze causes the inorganic things to flourish.

t_ch: 祂会庇佑垃圾桶吗？
t_en: Will THEY bless the trashcans?

t_ch: 当然，无论是金桶银桶垃圾桶还是马桶，桶王平等地看待所有子民。
t_en: Of course, regardless of whether they are golden cans, silver cans, trashcans, or dirty cans, the King of Cans treats all the people the same.

t_ch: 听着像是某个星神…
t_en: Sounds like an Aeon...

t_ch: 呃…在我们的教义里，星神也不过是桶王的化身，有相似之处很正常！
t_en: Ugh... In our teachings, Aeons are just incarnations of the King of Cans. Of course, they will have some similarities!

t_ch: 看到我身旁的这个铁桶了吗？这就是桶王在人间的圣物，是祂踏上「桶」之命途前锈蚀的神体。
t_en: Did you see the iron can beside me? It is the King of Cans' divine object left in the mortal realm, a rusted holy body before THEY embarked on THEIR destiny as a "Can."

t_ch: 只要诚心诚意地信仰桶王，你的任何愿望都会实现，所有疾病都会痊愈。哪怕是随便摸摸这个铁桶，神体也会为你带来好运。
t_en: By sincerely believing in the King of Cans, all your wishes will come true and all your diseases will be cured. Even if you are casually touching this iron can, THEIR holy body will bring you good luck.

t_ch: 要钱吗？
t_en: Do I need to pay?

t_ch: 免费就摸。
t_en: I'll touch if it's free.

t_ch: 肤浅！桶王是这样的神吗？祂是在拯救你们！金钱是邪恶的，你所遭遇的一切不幸，都来源于这份有毒的诅咒。
t_en: How shallow! Is that how you see our King of Cans? THEY are saving you! Money is the root of all evil. All your misfortune originates from that toxic curse.

t_ch: 所以桶王降下祂的神体，只要把钱投进这个铁桶，祂就会代你承受对应的苦难。
t_en: That's why the King of Cans made THEIR holy body descend. You just need to insert your money into this iron can, and THEY will bear the suffering on your behalf.

t_ch: 人也在祂的业务范围？
t_en: Do THEY bless humans too?

t_ch: 那还能有假？要不我这第一门徒的身份哪儿来的？
t_en: Do you not believe in me? How do you think I've become THEIR first disciple?

t_ch: 妈妈我要变成垃圾桶！
t_en: Mommy, I want to become a trashcan!

t_ch: 要的就是你这股热情！不过垃圾桶这个，确实要求有点高。
t_en: That's the enthusiasm I need! However, turning into a trashcan is a bit of a tall order.

t_ch: 这神还挺亲民。
t_en: What a friendly god.

t_ch: 没错，纵观整个银河，还有哪位神明像桶王一般视子民为己出。
t_en: Exactly. Throughout the entire Cosmos, which other gods regarded THEIR people as THEIR own like the King of Cans?

t_ch: 每个人其实都是桶，只是套上了人类的躯壳。当你走上铁桶命途的道路，就会褪去伪装，呈现出真实的姿态。
t_en: Every person is just a can wearing the shell of a human. The moment you embark onto the iron can's Path, your disguise will fade away, revealing your true self.

t_ch: 等全人类都变成了铁桶，真正的天堂就会降临。
t_en: When the entire human population becomes iron cans, the real paradise will descend.

t_ch: 来吧，走出你的第一步，让我大赚——我是说，成为桶王的第二位门徒。
t_en: Come on. Take your first step, and let me get rich — I mean, let me guide you to become the second disciple of the King.

t_ch: 只要<unbreak>1000</unbreak>苜蓿币，你就能更早地变成铁桶，抵达祂的身边。
t_en: All you need is to insert <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> Alfalfa credits into the can, and you'll be able to turn into an iron can and reach THEM earlier.

t_ch: 投点钱试试。
t_en: Give some money and see what happens.

t_ch: 没错，就是这样！我能看到，你身上的罪孽已被神体吞进了肚子。
t_en: Yes, that's it! I can see your sins have been swallowed into the holy body.

t_ch: 桶王的呼唤会在每夜伴你安详入眠。
t_en: The calling of the King of Cans will accompany you every night to a peaceful sleep.

t_ch: 还能再假点吗？
t_en: Can you be anymore fake?

t_ch: 你这不识好歹的家伙！侮辱我，就是在侮辱伟大的桶王。
t_en: What an ungrateful scoundrel! Insulting me is the same as insulting our mighty King of Cans.

t_ch: 也就是祂老人家慈悲为怀，否则你绝对看不到明天的太阳！
t_en: You should be thankful that THEY are compassionate and forgiving, or you will never see the sun tomorrow!

t_ch: 亲爱的朋友，愿桶王的光辉照耀你我。
t_en: My dear friend, may the light of the King of Cans shine upon you and me.

t_ch: 我编得应该够逼真了呀…再想办法改改。
t_en: My story should be credible enough... I should try to improve it.

t_ch: 真高兴见到你，桶王的第二门徒。
t_en: I am happy to see you, Second Disciple of the King of Cans.

t_ch: 瞧瞧眼前这令人喜悦的景象，人们摒弃了种种不净之物，投身于桶王门下。
t_en: Look at this delightful sight! Humans are discarding various impurities in their lives and devote themselves to the King of Cans.

t_ch: 我能感觉到，自己的身体正愈发接近祂的国度，头颅化作桶盖，躯体化作桶身。
t_en: I can feel my body gradually getting nearer to THEM. My head shall turn into a can lid, and my body shall become a can.

t_ch: 要再来摸摸神体吗？不再需要钱了，铁桶之神宽恕了我的罪行，赐予所有人触摸神体的权力。
t_en: Are you here to touch the holy body again? There's no need to insert credits now. The God of Iron Can has forgiven my sins and granted all humans the right to touch THEIR holy body.

t_ch: 你自己也信了？
t_en: Are you convinced too?

t_ch: 这是什么意思？难道你看不见吗？铁桶之神那投射在天空中的宏伟虚影。
t_en: What do you mean? Can't you see it? The grandiose silhouette of the God of Iron Can was projected in the sky.

t_ch: 你的骗术进步了。
t_en: You have improved on your tricks.

t_ch: 在桶王的福音下，我已经洗心革面，再也不会欺骗他人了。
t_en: With the teachings of the King of Cans, I've changed my ways and will never cheat others again.

t_ch: 真的吗？我不信。
t_en: Oh yeah? I don't believe you.

t_ch: 桶王并不会因为你的质疑就消失，祂是永恒的，并永恒地爱着我们。
t_en: The King of Cans will not disappear because of your disbelief. THEY are eternal and will love us forever.

t_ch: 我现在才知道，并非是我杜撰了这位神明——是我被选中了，所以才知晓了祂的一切！
t_en: Now I realize that it's not me who has chosen this God — I am the chosen one, and this is why I know everything about THEM.

t_ch: 他说的没错，我正是目睹了桶王的身姿，才从灵感匮乏里走出来，通过了编辑的审核！
t_en: This guy is right. I witnessed the form of the King of Cans, who rescued me from my lack of inspiration, and my work went through to the editor!

t_ch: 触摸了神体之后，我在赌场连赢了三回，又有钱买酒喝了。
t_en: After touching the holy body, I won three times in a row at the casino. I now have the money to buy booze again.

t_ch: 看啊，桶王听到了我们的赞美，祂正在注视我们！
t_en: Look, the King of Cans heard our praises for THEM. THEY are gazing at us!

t_ch: 那不是桶王。
t_en: That is not the King of Cans.

t_ch: 是不是桶王，我们看得一清二楚。莫要在这里妖言惑众，否则你必永堕桶底之狱。
t_en: We can tell with our own eyes whether that is the King of Cans. Stop spouting nonsense here, or you'll be punished at the bottom of the can for eternity.

t_ch: 你们这是魔怔了…
t_en: All of you are possessed...

t_ch: 非也，我们是觉醒了！从桶盖的缝隙中察觉到了世界的本质！
t_en: No. We are awakened! We can now perceive the nature of the world through the cracks in the lid of the can!

t_ch: 我才是桶王。
t_en: I am the King of Cans.

t_ch: 荒谬！你的外貌跟桶毫无关系，灵长目的古怪的身体怎能与桶王光滑完美的圆柱神躯相提并论！
t_en: Absurdity! Your appearance is nothing like a can. How could an odd-shaped primate body be compared to the King of Cans' smooth and perfect cylindrical body?

t_ch: 朋友，我对你很失望，竟然对我们的神如此不敬！
t_en: My friend, I am very disappointed in you. How can you show such disrespect to our God!?

t_ch: 但我不会谴责你对桶王的背叛，迷茫只是一时的，你终会通过这场试炼，回到桶王的怀抱。
t_en: But I will not condemn your betrayal of the King of Cans. Your confusion is only temporary. You'll eventually pass this trial and return to the arms of our King of Cans.

t_ch: 或许，你会成为比我更虔诚的信徒，成为一个优秀的铁桶，厉害的金桶，强大的垃圾桶！
t_en: Perhaps you'll become a more devout disciple than I am, becoming an excellent iron can, an astounding gold can, or even a powerful trashcan!

t_ch: 伟大的桶王！让我们变成铁桶吧！
t_en: Mighty King of Cans! Please turn us into iron cans!

t_ch: 伟大的桶王啊，让我变成铁桶吧！
t_en: Oh, mighty King of Cans. Turn me into an iron can!

t_ch: 这位{F#女士}{M#先生}，请留步，容我为您介绍一下我的神主，伟大的铁桶贤者，掌管世间财富的桶之神。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Sir}, please stay and allow me to introduce you to my Great One — the Sage of Iron Cans, the God of Cans who controls the wealth of the world.

t_ch: 祂有着绝美的面容，四只手臂分别掌管金属、纸币、货物和能源。这个铁桶，便是掌管金属的那只手亲自赐福的神体。
t_en: THEY have a stunningly charming face, and THEIR four arms are in charge of metal, paper money, goods, and energy. And this iron can is the holy body blessed by the arm that governs metal.

t_ch: 换了个神？
t_en: Have you changed a god?

t_ch: 版本更新了？
t_en: A new update patch?

t_ch: 是你呀，一下子没认出来。
t_en: Oh, it's you! I didn't recognize you for a second.

t_ch: 多亏有你的点拨，我意识到自己编故事的水平确实欠佳，桶王这神听起来就不太好骗——赚钱。
t_en: Thank you for your feedback. I realized that I was awful at making up stories, and the King of Cans doesn't sound like a good scam... for making money.

t_ch: 所以我总结了经验教训，借鉴了现存星神的形象和宣传词，连夜赶制了这个全新版本。人形，好看，人气绝对低不了。
t_en: So I've summarized the lessons learned from my experience, and with reference to the existing Aeons' images and promotional messages, I burned the midnight oil to come out with this all-new version. With this charming appearance, their popularity can never be low.

t_ch: 搁这儿卖偶像呢。
t_en: You sound like you're advertising for an idol.

t_ch: 偶像这是市场需求，市场你懂吗？大家就好这口。
t_en: An idol is what the market demands. Do you understand what's the market? Everyone likes this.

t_ch: 也不怕真信徒来削你。
t_en: Are you not afraid the true disciples will crack down on you?

t_ch: 家族的人要是能追杀我到这儿来，我倒立喝苏乐达给你看。
t_en: If the members of The Family are capable of coming here to punish me, I would drink SoulGlad upside down for you.

t_ch: 我回去也捏个球棒神。
t_en: I should go home and make a Baseballer God too.

t_ch: 那是什么玩意儿，听着就感觉不如铁桶贤者霸气。
t_en: What kind of nonsense is that? It certainly doesn't sound as mighty as my Sage of Iron Cans.

t_ch: 说到底，敬畏之心这种东西，我早丢掉了，只要能赚钱，改信扑满我都愿意。
t_en: At the end of the day, reverence is something I lost a long time ago. I'm even willing to believe in Trotters if that can bring me money.

t_ch: 你就想想，同样是铁桶之神，一个长得堪比银河巨星，一个长得像个垃圾桶，你更容易上谁的当？
t_en: Think about it. If you have two God of Iron Cans, and one looks like a cosmic superstar while the other looks like a typical trashcan. Which one are you more likely to believe in?

t_ch: 银河巨星。
t_en: The cosmic superstar.

t_ch: 对吧！我们铁桶之神，也是要注重公众形象的。
t_en: Right! Even as a God of Iron Can, public image is important too.

t_ch: 那必是垃圾桶。
t_en: The trashcan, of course.

t_ch: 额，抛开部分特殊爱好，整体来说银河巨星还是更受欢迎的。
t_en: Eh, putting unique hobbies aside, a cosmic superstar is still more popular overall.

t_ch: 总之，诈骗就像炒股，一个路数行不通就得换一个更吃香的。
t_en: In any case, scams are like buying stocks. If one stock doesn't work for you, just change to another one.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，腰缠万贯的明天已经在向我招手了。
t_en: Hehe, I can already see tomorrow's loaded wallet beckoning me.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，腰缠万贯的明天已经在向我招手了。
t_en: Hehe, I can already see tomorrow's loaded wallet beckoning me.

t_ch: 桶王带走了祂的信徒，只留下一片死寂。
t_en: The King of Cans left with THEIR disciples, leaving an atmosphere of profound stillness.

t_ch: 原始梦境并不稳定，迷因的诞生和死去都更加频繁，在信徒们都失去意识后，大概会很快消亡吧。
t_en: The Primordial Dreamscape isn't stable, yet the frequency of the births and deaths of memes is increasing. Once the disciples have all lost consciousness, they will probably all dissipate very rapidly.

t_ch: 或许过段日子，你就能在哪里看到一大群失忆的人突然出现的新闻。
t_en: Maybe you'll see the news reporting about a large group of people with amnesia suddenly appearing somewhere on the street sometime later.

t_ch: 在梦的深处，九千只眼睛的桶王沉睡着，等待着全人类变成铁桶的那天。
t_en: In the depth of the dream, the nine-thousand-eyed King of Cans slumbers, waiting for the day when all of mankind is turned into iron cans.

t_ch: 你在铁桶内发现了一个小小的梦泡，不知道是哪个帮派的手笔。
t_en: In the iron can, you discover a tiny dream bubble. You wonder which lineage made it.

t_ch: 触摸到它的瞬间，微妙的信息也跟着传入你的脑海。
t_en: The moment you touch it, a subtle message is delivered into your head.

t_ch: 上面记录了「桶的令使」的诸多罪行，包括对流梦礁居民行诈、造谣「钟表匠」长得和桶一样、在酒吧赊账不还等共计十余条内容。
t_en: The message records the dozens of crimes committed by the Emanator of Cans, including extorting money from the Dreamflux Reef residents, spreading rumors that the Watchmaker's appearance is similar to that of a can, and failing to pay the bills at the bar.

t_ch: 想不到这小子还挺「多才多艺」的，呵，真是罪有应得。
t_en: It seems like that fellow is pretty "talented." Welp, he's getting what he deserves.

t_ch: 赞美伟大的桶王！
t_en: Praise the mighty King of Cans!

t_ch: 怎么还没人过来…
t_en: Why isn't anyone coming here...

t_ch: 伟大的桶王！
t_en: Mighty King of Cans!

t_ch: 我要变成铁桶！
t_en: I want to turn into an iron can!

t_ch: 看来，他们已经完全被自己创造的迷因蛊惑住了。
t_en: Look, they are completely obsessed with the memes created by their own.

t_ch: 由众人意识产生的铁桶「神」，祂会成长到何种规模？过段时间再回来看看吧。
t_en: Arising from the consciousness of the people, just how big will this "God" of Iron Cans become? Perhaps you should check back in a while.

t_ch: 所以，为什么一定要执着于铁桶这个元素？
t_en: Then again, why must these people be obsessed over the idea of iron cans?

t_ch: 灰毛{F#小妞}{M#小子}，站住！
t_en: Hey, gray hair {F#chick}{M#brat}, stop right there!

t_ch: 不等你有所反应，角落里立刻蹦出来几个人将你团团围住。
t_en: Before you have a chance to react, a group of people jump out from around the corner and surround you.

t_ch: 湮厄鹰来了拔毛，赤羽狼过了剥皮——「流梦帮」在此！
t_en: We'd pluck the Torment Eagles bald if they cross here, and skin the Redfeather Wolves if they dare to pass... We are the Dreamflux Gang!

t_ch: 到了我们的地盘，是走是留，你说的可就不算了——过路费，<unbreak>2000</unbreak>苜蓿币。
t_en: You're on our turf. To stay or go ain't your call no more... You gotta pay the toll. Hand over <unbreak>2,000</unbreak> Alfalfa credits.

t_ch: 这地方写了是你家的？
t_en: Do you have that ownership in writing?

t_ch: 当然，墙上的涂鸦就是我们的产权证明。
t_en: Of course. The graffiti on these walls is our property deed.

t_ch: 能办月卡吗？
t_en: Can I buy a monthly subscription?

t_ch: 啊？你当这里是苜蓿草开的游乐园吗？{F#小妞}{M#小子}，我们是在打劫！
t_en: Huh? You think this is some kinda amusement park run by the Alfalfa? This is a robbery, {F#girl}{M#boy}!

t_ch: 流梦礁可是「原初梦境」，你要是在这儿出了事，可别想像美梦里一样全身而退。
t_en: Remember, Dreamflux Reef is the Primordial Dreamscape... If ya get into trouble here, don't think ya'll be gettin' out without a scratch like in a dream.

t_ch: 我了解你们这些闯进来的宾客，两千对你们根本算不上什么，能用这点钱买来平安，是笔很划算的买卖。
t_en: I know you intrudin' travelers. Two thousand ain't nothin' to you! A little bit of petty change for safety ain't a bad deal if you ask me.

t_ch: 你也可以反抗，不过到那时——命和钱，你一个也留不下！
t_en: Or you could fight back. But, if you do... then ya won't have money or a life!

t_ch: 好汉饶命，我这就给！
t_en: Spare my life, sir! Here is your money!

t_ch: 明智的选择——苜蓿币不方便的话，我们也支持信用点支付。
t_en: A wise decision... If Alfalfa credits ain't no good for ya, we can take IPC credit payments, too.

t_ch: 识相的{F#小妞}{M#小子}，期待你的下次光临。
t_en: We look forward to your next visit, smart {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 能反抗？早说啊。
t_en: I can fight back? Why didn't you say so earlier?

t_ch: 梦泡电影看多了，真以为你一个人能打过我们流梦帮？
t_en: You've been watchin' too many dream bubble movies! Ya really think one person can beat the Dreamflux Gang?

t_ch: 大家一起上，给{F#她}{M#他}好好讲讲这儿的规矩。
t_en: Get' em you guys! Looks like we need to teach {F#her}{M#him} how things work 'round here!

t_ch: 我是来加入这个家的！
t_en: I came to join this family!

t_ch: 难道你也是无家可归的流浪者？唔…按我们帮派的规矩，应该给你个机会。
t_en: You're a homeless vagrant, too? Uhh... The gang's rules say we gotta give you a chance.

t_ch: 想加入流梦帮，就要先学会我们独有的一套暗号——别被吓跑了，我们的考核可是很严的。
t_en: If ya wanna join the Dreamflux Gang, ya have to learn our secret signal... Our assessments are super strict y'know, don't go runnin' off now!

t_ch: 识相的{F#小妞}{M#小子}，期待你的下次光临。
t_en: We look forward to your next visit, smart {F#girl}{M#boy}.

t_ch: 打劫——一口价，<unbreak>3000</unbreak>苜蓿币，不接受砍价。
t_en: This is a robbery... Fixed price, <unbreak>3,000</unbreak> Alfalfa credits, no barterin'.

t_ch: 这也能杀熟？
t_en: Wait, even robbers trick their friends?

t_ch: 我们向来秉承公平打劫的原则，绝不区别对待任何的过路人。
t_en: We uphold the principle of fair robbery and never give any passerby special treatment.

t_ch: 我上次交过了。
t_en: I paid the last time.

t_ch: 上次是上次，现在是现在，你订了昨天的白日梦酒店，今天还能继续住吗？
t_en: That was before, this is now. If ya booked a room at The Reverie yesterday, ya think ya can stay there again today?

t_ch: 怎么涨价了？
t_en: How come the price has gone up?

t_ch: 上次打劫后，我们学习了同行的先进经验，对单次额度进行了一定的上调。
t_en: After the last robbery, we learned from the experience of our peers n' increased our quota by a set value.

t_ch: 都是老熟人了，我也不多说什么，赶快交钱走人，大家都轻松。
t_en: We know each other by now, I ain't gonna keep explainin'. Pay the money n' scram n' we can all relax.

t_ch: 不就三千，我有的是钱！
t_en: Alright, <unbreak>3,000</unbreak>. If I've got anything, it's money!

t_ch: 回头客就是爽快，这次还是信用点支付可以吧？
t_en: Return customers are the best. Are you okay with a credit payment this time?

t_ch: 多谢惠顾。
t_en: Thank you for your custom.

t_ch: 忍无可忍，战！
t_en: Enough is enough. Bring it on!

t_ch: 呵，梦泡电影看多了，真以为你一个人能打过我们？
t_en: You've been watchin' too many dream bubble movies! Ya really think ya can beat us alone?

t_ch: 大家一起上，给{F#她}{M#他}好好讲讲这儿的规矩。
t_en: Get' em you guys! Looks like we need to teach {F#her}{M#him} how things work 'round here!

t_ch: 我是来加入这个家的！
t_en: I came to join this family!

t_ch: 是这样么？原来你也是无家可归的人，这…给你个机会吧。
t_en: Oh? So you're a homeless vagrant, too? Then... we'll give ya a chance.

t_ch: 想加入帮派，就要先学会我们独有的一套暗号——别被吓跑了，我们的考核可是很严的。
t_en: If you wanna join the Dreamflux Gang, ya have to learn our secret signal... Our assessments are super strict y'know, don't go runnin' off now!

t_ch: 多谢惠顾。
t_en: Thank you for your custom.

t_ch: 打劫——一口价，<unbreak>10000</unbreak>苜蓿币，不接受砍价。
t_en: This is a robbery... Fixed price, <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> Alfalfa credits, no barterin'.

t_ch: 忍无可忍，战！
t_en: Enough is enough. Bring it on!

t_ch: 呵，梦泡电影看多了，真以为你一个人能打过我们？
t_en: You've been watchin' too many dream bubble movies! Ya really think ya can beat us alone?

t_ch: 大家一起上，给{F#她}{M#他}好好讲讲这儿的规矩。
t_en: Get' em you guys! Looks like we need to teach {F#her}{M#him} how things work 'round here!

t_ch: 别打了，别打了，再打就要痛醒了。
t_en: Stop, stop! Beat us any harder n' we'll be beaten awake!

t_ch: 英雄，挨了你这一顿胖揍我算明白了，为什么我们流梦帮的业务一直没法做大做强——就是缺少一位和你一样智慧与力量并存的靠山。
t_en: O hero, this beatin' ya gave us has made me realize why the Dreamflux Gang's business ain't able to get bigger n' stronger... It's 'cos we're missin' a powerful n' intelligent backer like you!

t_ch: 在此，我诚恳地邀请你加入我们，让流梦帮的事业更上一层楼。
t_en: I'd like to sincerely invite ya to join us n' help the Dreamflux Gang's business reach the next level.

t_ch: 我很忙的。
t_en: I'm very busy.

t_ch: 不会耽误你太多时间，挂个名给我们撑场面就行。
t_en: It won't take up much of ya time! All ya gotta do is say ya associate with us n' give us some street cred!

t_ch: 有点意思。
t_en: Not bad.

t_ch: 对吧，而且我们也不是天天都在抢劫，平时也会一起打打牌、玩玩以太战线、看看钟表小子什么的。
t_en: Right? Plus we ain't robbin' people every day. Sometimes we play cards or Aetherium Wars together or just watch Clockie n' stuff...

t_ch: 打不过就招安？
t_en: If you can't beat them, recruit them?

t_ch: 不不不，是被你的个人魅力与实力深深地折服了！
t_en: No, no, it's not that! We are simply just bowled over by ya strength n' charm!

t_ch: 不过，想加入帮派，需要学会我们独有的一套暗号。即使你是特邀成员，这个规矩也不能跳过。
t_en: Although, if ya wanna join the gang, ya still gotta learn our secret signal. This rule can't be skipped, even if ya were invited.

t_ch: 我会先向你演示一遍，很难的，请一定要记住。
t_en: I'll demonstrate it for ya. It's real hard, so make sure ya memorize it.

t_ch: 双手抱胸。
t_en: Cross yer arms.

t_ch: 摊开手。
t_en: Spread 'em out.

t_ch: 双手叉腰。
t_en: Put your hands on your hips.

t_ch: 这就完了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 哼哼，很有特色吧？我设计的时候花了小半天呢。
t_en: Heh, pretty unique, right? Took me half a day to come up with that!

t_ch: 你是不是故意放水？
t_en: Did you make it easy on purpose?

t_ch: 呃…一定是你的错觉，我们的测试可是很难的。
t_en: Ughh... Ya must be mistaken. Our test is real hard.

t_ch: 你们帮派是早教班？
t_en: Is this gang also a kindergarten?

t_ch: 我以前确实在这里当老师…哎，你能学明白再说吧。
t_en: Well, I did use to be a teacher here... Hey, come back when ya manage to learn it.

t_ch: 你可以先记一记，记好了我们就开始吧。
t_en: Take a moment to memorize, then we'll start when you're ready.

t_ch: 抱胸，摊手，双手叉腰——记住了吗？让我们开始考试吧。
t_en: Cross yer arms, spread 'em out, hands on your hips — you got that? Let's start the exam now.

t_ch: 双手叉腰。
t_en: Put your hands on your hips.

t_ch: 做我们这行也是要靠点脑子的，你要不再好好想想顺序？
t_en: Ya need brains for our line of work too, ya know. Ya wanna have another think 'bout the order?

t_ch: 什么也不做。
t_en: Do nothing.

t_ch: 虽然你这幅样子很适合去撑场子，但现在是做暗号的时候吧！
t_en: That face ya got might give us street cred, but now it's time to do the secret signal!

t_ch: 抱胸。
t_en: Cross your arms over your chest.

t_ch: 嗯，做的不错，充分传达了我们对家族的愤恨！
t_en: Yeah, not bad! It really conveys our rage toward The Family!

t_ch: 下一个动作，你可以的！
t_en: Next move. Ya got this!

t_ch: 双手叉腰。
t_en: Put your hands on your hips.

t_ch: 这是下一个动作，你急什么？
t_en: That's the one after. What're ya rushin' for?

t_ch: 刷会儿手机。
t_en: Play with your phone.

t_ch: 同学，考试的时候不要掏手机出来！
t_en: Students should know there ain't no playin' with phones durin' an exam!

t_ch: 摊开手。
t_en: Spread 'em out.

t_ch: 很好，短短一个动作，就诠释出了我们对流梦礁现状的无奈……
t_en: Real nice. That short, simple gesture expresses our helplessness at the current state of Dreamflux Reef...

t_ch: 你是我教过最好的学生，最后一个动作了，加油！
t_en: You're the best student I've ever taught! Last one. Ya got this!

t_ch: 睡觉。
t_en: Sleep.

t_ch: 我们不是在梦里吗？你怎么睡得着的？
t_en: Aren't we in a dream? How're ya gonna sleep?

t_ch: 沉默装酷。
t_en: Stay quiet and act cool.

t_ch: 现在是耍帅的时候吗？
t_en: Is this really the time to try to be cool?

t_ch: 双手叉腰。
t_en: Put your hands on your hips.

t_ch: 叉腰，既是表现我们的自信，也是勉励自己——无论明天是什么样子，我们都无所畏惧。
t_en: Hands on hips expresses our confidence while also encouraging ourselves — regardless of what tomorrow brings, we'll meet it head-on.

t_ch: 也不知道该不该说恭喜，现在你也是流梦帮的一员了。有什么需要的，都可以来找我们，咱们帮派得互帮互助才行。
t_en: I'm not sure if I should say congratulations, but yer now a member of the Dreamflux Gang. If ya need anythin', come find us. We gang members gotta help each other out!

t_ch: 也不知道该不该说恭喜，现在你也是流梦帮的一员了。有什么需要的，都可以来找我们，咱们帮派得互帮互助才行。
t_en: I'm not sure if I should say congratulations, but yer now a member of the Dreamflux Gang. If ya need anythin', come find us. We gang members gotta help each other out!

t_ch: 你来啦？
t_en: You here?

t_ch: 抱胸、摊手、双手叉腰。
t_en: Cross arms, arms spread, hands on hips.

t_ch: 看来你已经很熟悉我们的暗号了。
t_en: Looks like you're now real familiar with the secret signal.

t_ch: 双手叉腰、摊手、抱胸。
t_en: Hands on hips, arms spread, cross arms.

t_ch: 做错了，你还是得多多学习啊。
t_en: That ain't right. Ya gotta study a bit more!

t_ch: 我就静静看着。
t_en: Stare silently.

t_ch: 咱们虽然生活落魄，但仪式感还是要有的。
t_en: We may be pretty down'n'out, but we still need to have a sense of ceremony, y'know.

t_ch: 别看我们现在这么不靠谱，流梦帮以前可是这条街的守护者——那时的流梦礁还有很多居民，大家自发地组成帮派，响应「钟表匠」的号召守卫流梦礁的自由。
t_en: Don't judge us by what we look like now. The Dreamflux Gang used to be the guardians of this street... There were loads of citizens in Dreamflux Reef back then, so we responded to the call of the Watchmaker n' formed a gang on our own to protect the freedom of Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 我们帮派当初都是被美梦放逐的犯人，但「钟表匠」却接纳了我们，还帮我们找到了谋生的工作……
t_en: Our gang was made up of convicts who had been exiled by the dream world. But the Watchmaker accepted us and helped us find jobs to make a living...

t_ch: 托他的福，有人开了面包店、有人当了服务员、而我成了个教人防身的老师…呵呵，但你看看我现在的样子，已经不配被称为老师了。
t_en: Thanks to him, some people opened bakeries, some became waiters, and I became a self-defense teacher... Hah, but look at me now, I ain't worthy to call myself a teacher no more.

t_ch: 但美梦对流梦礁的侵蚀每天都在加剧，我们短暂的幸福逐渐化作泡影，那位「钟表匠」也…随着流梦礁逐渐破败，大家都纷纷离开了这里。
t_en: But the dream's corrosion of Dreamflux Reef got worse by the day... Our short-lived happiness gradually transformed into empty bubbles. Then, the Watchmaker... Well, everyone started leavin' as Dreamflux Reef started to fall apart.

t_ch: 没了客人，没了工作，没了家，我们就成了流浪街头的一窝老鼠，只能靠敲诈勒索为生。像我们这样的流浪者，在流梦礁比比皆是。
t_en: No customers, no work, no homes... We became a nest of rats relyin' on extortion to survive. Vagrants like us are all over the place in Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 你说，现在的流梦礁，真的还有药可救吗？
t_en: Do you think there's anythin' we can do to save Dreamflux Reef now?

t_ch: 可以先整合人手。
t_en: You should unify the people first.

t_ch: 你说得对…如果我们能集合所有在城区流浪的居民，或许就能改变这里的现状。
t_en: You're right... If we can gather all the residents roamin' the city, maybe we can change things 'round here.

t_ch: 可以先整合物资。
t_en: You should gather your resources first.

t_ch: 的确，把分散的资源集中起来，就能有效降低流浪生活的成本。
t_en: You're right. If we gather our scattered resources, we can effectively lower the cost of life.

t_ch: 你说的很有道理，我们也可以用涂鸦来宣传自己的理念，但启动资金怎么办？
t_en: You've got a point. We could also use our graffiti to spread our ideals. But how're we gonna get the funds to kick the whole thing off?

t_ch: 做梦泡赚钱！
t_en: Make dream bubbles to earn money!

t_ch: 和其他帮派抢梦泡市场？我们几个都上过学，如果条件允许，做出的产品应该可以达到安全出售的合法标准。
t_en: Compete with other gangs on the dream bubble market? We've all been to school... If the conditions are right, the stuff we make can probably meet the legal safety standards for sale.

t_ch: 去现实世界赚钱。
t_en: Go to the real world to earn money.

t_ch: 对哦，干嘛只把视线放在梦里呢？去现实赚了钱再回来，虽然苦了点，但总好过抢劫。
t_en: You're right. Why are we puttin' all our focus only on the dream world? Going back to the real world to earn cash might be a bit more work, but it's better than robbin'!

t_ch: 向居民募捐。
t_en: Ask the residents for donations.

t_ch: 这…
t_en: That's...

t_ch: 劫富济贫！
t_en: Steal from the rich to give to the poor!

t_ch: 劫富…伙计，这块地界的人都穷得响叮当，一条街的全部家当都比不上一位贵宾的钱包——还是再想想有没有其他办法吧。
t_en: The rich... Buddy, everyone in this area is dirt poor. Ya could add up the money of everyone on one street n' they ain't even gonna match a VIP's wallet... We might need another approach...

t_ch: 经你这么一说，我终于想明白了，太感谢你了！
t_en: After talkin' to ya, I finally know what to do. I'm so grateful!

t_ch: 伙计，你不仅没有因为抢劫记恨我们，反而给大家指了一条明路…在你身上，我看到了「钟表匠」先生当年的影子！
t_en: Buddy, not only didn't ya hate us for robbin' ya, ya even guided us on the right path... Seein' ya now, I can see mister Watchmaker inside ya!

t_ch: 不管你同不同意，从今往后，你就是我们流梦帮的老大了！
t_en: Whether ya agree or not, from now on, you're gonna be the Dreamflux Gang's boss!

t_ch: 老大！老大！老大！
t_en: Boss! Boss! Boss!

t_ch: 老大，我们已经商量过了未来的计划，很快就能付诸实践了。
t_en: Boss, we've been discussin' our future plans, n' we're almost ready to put'em into practice.

t_ch: 老大，你终于回来了！根据你的建议，我们已经从抢劫犯毕业了！
t_en: Boss, yer finally back! Thanks to yer suggestion, we've finally moved on from bein' robbers!

t_ch: 赚到钱之后，我们也拾起了以前的工作，去帮居民解决生活问题，抓捕偷鸡摸狗的犯人，甚至来打扫这条街道的卫生…大家也不会觉得我们是坏人了。
t_en: Once we got our cash, we went back to our old jobs. We helped the residents deal with their problems, captured all the pesky criminals n' even cleaned up the street... People don't think we're bad guys no more, either!

t_ch: 很多无家可归的居民看到了我们的成功，也打算加入其中——以后，这里就是所有流浪者们的家了，我们会重拾曾经的责任，维护流梦礁的和平与安全。
t_en: A load of the homeless locals have seen our success n' wanna join us too... In the future, this'll be a home for all vagrants. We will take up our old responsibility n' maintain the peace n' safety of Dreamflux Reef!

t_ch: 那位米凯先生说，如果「钟表匠」看到了这一切，一定会为我们感到骄傲的…这都是老大你的功劳！
t_en: That Micah guy said that if the Watchmaker could see us now, he'd be real proud of us... This is all thanks to you, boss!

t_ch: 为了感谢老大，我们特地用赚到的第一桶金给你买了份礼物…东西很便宜，还希望你不要嫌弃。
t_en: To thank you, we used our first profits to buy ya a gift... It ain't nothin' fancy, so we hope ya don't mind.

t_ch: 老大，有空了记得常回来看看，大伙都很想你！
t_en: Boss, make sure ya come back n' see us when ya get time! We're all gonna miss ya!

t_ch: 我数到三，一起上…
t_en: Everyone with me on three...

t_ch: 老大，我们整合了几条街的资源，办了个小学校，我又能给孩子们上课了！
t_en: Boss, we pulled together resources from the whole block n' put together a little school! I can teach kids again!

t_ch: 虽然现在还没几个学生，但以后肯定会越来越多的。
t_en: There ain't many students at the moment, but there will only be more n' more in the future!

t_ch: 老大，你不在的这段时间，我的厨艺可是大有进步，连隔壁街的奶奶吃了都说好。
t_en: Boss, my cookin's really improved since ya been away. Even that old lady on the next street over said it was good!

t_ch: 等有空了，你也来尝尝我的手艺。
t_en: You gotta come try my cookin' when ya get a minute!

t_ch: 老大，米凯先生说，如果我们缺什么东西，都可以找他商量。
t_en: Boss, Mr. Micah said that if we need anythin', we can go talk to him.

t_ch: 你说，咱们现在是不是成正规组织了？
t_en: Does that mean we're like a proper organization now?

t_ch: 老大好，我是新加入的流浪者，以前在这片地界搞点小偷小摸的营生。
t_en: Hey, boss! I'm a new member. I used to get up to some petty theft 'round these parts.

t_ch: 不得不说，作为一个守法公民，重新得到尊重的感觉可真好。
t_en: I gotta say, it feels real good bein' a law-abidin' citizen n' winnin' back the respect of others.

t_ch: 老大好，初次见面，我是垃圾场的拾荒者，大家都叫我「小旋风」。
t_en: Hey, boss! Nice to meet ya. I'm a scavenger at the garbage dump. Everyone calls me "Little Tornado."

t_ch: 你有没有发现，周围的臭味都消失了？那可都是我的功劳哦。
t_en: Don't know if ya noticed that the stench 'round here has disappeared? That was my hard work!

t_ch: 啊！这位{F#女士}{M#先生}，请允许我直视你的光辉，你的身躯比超新星还要耀眼，你的美德就连旅过千星的苦修者也无法企及。
t_en: Ah! {F#Madam}{M#Sir}, please allow me to get a good look at your brilliance! Your body is more dazzling than a supernova! Not even an ascetic who has traveled many stars could aspire to match your virtue!

t_ch: 一看到你，我的心里就像下了场两百万年的雨，苦寒的坚冰也化作生机的雨林。
t_en: One look at you and it feels like two million years of rain has fallen on my heart, and what was once bitterly cold and frozen has thawed to form a vibrant rainforest!

t_ch: 能在此时此刻相见于此地，想必也是神主希佩在可怜我的遭遇。
t_en: Meeting you here, I am certain that the Great One Xipe is taking pity on me for my tough times.

t_ch: 有话直说。
t_en: What do you want?

t_ch: 有气快放。
t_en: Get to the point or put a cap on it.

t_ch: 好一位心直口快的英豪，那我也长话短说——你听过「苏乐达海选」吗？
t_en: Such a direct and outspoken hero! Then I shall make a long story short... Have you heard of the SoulGlad Auditions?

t_ch: 这场海选在我们汽水界，就像你们的「盛会海选」一样有名。
t_en: The SoulGlad Auditions are to the soda world what the Festivity Auditions are to you.

t_ch: 全匹诺康尼最具优秀的苏乐达齐聚一堂，在疯狂的厮杀中选拔出「巨星苏乐达」。
t_en: The most outstanding SoulGlads in all of Penacony are gathered together and engage in a vicious battle to find the SoulGlad Superstar.

t_ch: 我本应是这届海选当之无愧的第一名，可我的死对头花了重金，在赛前请了一打包装精致的玫瑰小酒杯，诱惑我打开了瓶盖，将汽水倒入温柔乡中……
t_en: I was a shoo-in for first place this year, but one of my arch-competitors spent a fortune and hired a crate of fine rose wine bottles that seduced me into popping my cap, and pouring all of my contents into the embrace of sweetness...

t_ch: 等我反应过来，我最大的竞争优势——强悍的炸裂气泡已经空空如也了！
t_en: When I realized what had happened, my biggest competitive edge — my powerful bursting bubbles — were all gone!

t_ch: {F#好姐姐}{M#好哥哥}，大赛在即，你忍心看到一瓶心怀高远的苏乐达就此折戟吗？
t_en: {F#Kind lady}{M#Kind sir}, the competition is imminent, can you really bear to see a SoulGlad with such lofty aspirations fall flat like this?

t_ch: 给你买台加压器？
t_en: You want me to buy you a pressurizer?

t_ch: 给你买个打气机？
t_en: You want me to buy you a gas pump?

t_ch: 现在谈这些已经来不及了，没气的苏乐达就只是瓶发腻的糖浆，但我有个古典主义的方法。
t_en: There's no time for such things now. A bottle of SoulGlad that has lost its fizz is nothing more than a bottle of gross syrup. But, I have a more traditional solution.

t_ch: 你来骂我，骂得越难听越好。
t_en: I want you to insult me, the harsher the better!

t_ch: 当愤怒达到顶点，压力膨胀，大量的气体就会从我的瓶中喷涌而出。靠着这股怒气，我便会获得无尽的气泡，一举拿下海选的桂冠。
t_en: When my anger reaches its peak, the pressure will build up and a huge amount of air will come bursting forth from my bottle. With this rage, I'll obtain infinite bubbles and snatch the audition crown!

t_ch: 你这要求挺别致啊。
t_en: That's a pretty unique request.

t_ch: 尽管来吧，正面骂我！
t_en: Bring it on. Insult me to my face!

t_ch: 这不太好吧…
t_en: That doesn't sound like a good idea...

t_ch: 为了前途，我没意见！
t_en: This is for my future. I have no problem!

t_ch: 那我就不客气了！
t_en: Then I won't hold back!

t_ch: 等等，我得先整理一下仪表——作为未来的巨星苏乐达，挨骂也不能缺少仪式感。
t_en: Hold on, let me groom myself a little... As the future SoulGlad Superstar, I must learn to face even insults with a sense of ceremony.

t_ch: 苏乐达瓶子狰狞地扭动了几下，但你完全看不出变化。
t_en: The SoulGlad bottle wriggles about grotesquely a few times, but you can't see a difference at all.

t_ch: 这种「看起来做了什么，但其实什么也没做」的做法，可能也是一种仪式感。
t_en: Perhaps this method of "looking like it's doing something but actually did nothing" is also part of the ceremony?

t_ch: 它自豪地挺起胸膛，仅剩的一点气泡咕噜噜地转动，示意你可以开始骂它了。
t_en: It puffs out its chest proudly. Its remaining few bubbles stir with a gurgle to indicate that it is ready for your insults.

t_ch: {F#姐}{M#哥}，我已经做好觉悟了，让咱们速战速决。
t_en: {F#Madam}{M#Sir}, I am prepared. Let us make this a swift battle!

t_ch: 准备面对地狱吧！
t_en: Get ready for a taste of hell!

t_ch: 我，无所畏惧！
t_en: I... I fear nothing!

t_ch: 别急，再等等。
t_en: Wait a second. There's no rush.

t_ch: 可别太久，海选马上就要开始了。
t_en: Do not make me wait too long. The auditions are about to begin.

t_ch: 可千万别心软，放开了骂。我能不能走进收藏展柜，成为「巨星苏乐达」，就全靠你的嘴皮子了。
t_en: Don't go getting all soft on me. Give me your worst! Whether I can enter the collection display cabinet and become the SoulGlad Superstar is counting on that mouth of yours!

t_ch: 你知道的，我们苏乐达的情绪都比较容易激动。所以，无论中途我出了什么问题，都不要停下来，愤怒到什么程度才能取胜，我心里有数。
t_en: As you know, us SoulGlads' emotions are quite easily stimulated. So, no matter what happens to me, you must not stop! I know how mad I have to be to take victory!

t_ch: 来吧，让我接受你枪林弹雨的洗礼。
t_en: Come, let me feel your baptism of bullets!

t_ch: 你这…
t_en: You little...

t_ch: 慢慢慢慢，你再缓缓，我先深呼吸一下——再深呼吸一下，苏萨先生保佑，艾迪恩老板保佑。
t_en: Wait, wait, wait, wait. Slow down. Let me take a deep breath... And another one... May Mr. Sousa bless me, may Boss Aideen bless me.

t_ch: 好了，重新开始吧。
t_en: Alright, let's start again.

t_ch: 你什么档次，值得我来骂？
t_en: You think cheap trash like you is worth my insults?

t_ch: 不是说好的吗？这时候出尔反尔算什么意思？卑鄙无耻的人类！
t_en: I thought we had an agreement? Are you saying you're going to go back on your word after all this? You wretched, shameless human!

t_ch: 都说人心似水，这话简直是对水最大的侮辱——看到这些气泡了吗？它们代表我对你的愤怒和蔑视！
t_en: They say the hearts of humans are like water... But I'd say that's an insult to water! You see these bubbles? They represent my rage and contempt for you!

t_ch: 等等，这些气泡…原来这就是你的计策啊，我悟了！
t_en: Wait, these bubbles... So that was your plan all along? I get it!

t_ch: 你还能洗马桶吗？
t_en: Can you still be used to clean a toilet?

t_ch: 如果已经没气的话。
t_en: If you've got no fizz left in you, that is.

t_ch: 说过多少次了，苏乐达洗马桶完全是种族歧视，是对我们的污名化。
t_en: How many times have I said this? SoulGlad being used to wash toilets is a racist stereotype! It's defamation of our name!

t_ch: 什么东西不能洗马桶？怎么不说用反物质炸弹洗呢？干净得连马桶都不剩。
t_en: What can't be used to clean a toilet? Why not use an anti-matter grenade? That'll get it so clean there won't even be a toilet left!

t_ch: 该死，看看这些全新的气泡，虽然你这招很老套，但效果确实不错。
t_en: Darn it, look at these fresh bubbles. That may not have been very original, but it was effective.

t_ch: 你喜欢什么口味？
t_en: What flavor do you like the most?

t_ch: 我？当然是冰点苏乐达，那完美地停留在冻结前一刻的危险体验。
t_en: Me? Ice SoulGlad, obviously. The danger of staying in that state just before you freeze completely is perfect!

t_ch: 那滋味，光是想想就…不对，你得骂我啊，得骂我！
t_en: That flavor, just thinking about it makes me... No, wait. You're supposed to insult me! Insult me!

t_ch: 现在我连全盛的万分之一都达不到，再来！
t_en: I'm not even one ten-thousandth of my full fizz right now. Try again!

t_ch: 苏乐达企业真是没良心。
t_en: The SoulGlad company is heartless.

t_ch: 骂我就骂我，说到我的生产商头上就过分了，苏乐达招你惹你了？
t_en: If I say insult me, then insult me! Bringing my manufacturer into this is going too far. What did SoulGlad even do to you?

t_ch: 苏乐达企业可是匹诺康尼首屈一指的惠民企业，产品在数千年间都保持物美价廉，有没有良心你说的可不算！
t_en: The SoulGlad enterprise is one of the top public benefactors in Penacony! Their products have maintained excellent quality with low prices for millennia, and you say they are heartless!?

t_ch: ——哇！气泡涨了好多，瓶盖都要给我气飞了！好啊，你骂得好啊！
t_en: ...Woah! That's got my bubbles going! My bottle cap is ready to burst! Alright, you know how to insult someone!

t_ch: 喝苏乐达考不上折纸大学！
t_en: You'll never get into Paperfold University by drinking SoulGlad!

t_ch: 怎么啥事都能怪苏乐达啊？考不上折纸大学跟饮料有啥关系？
t_en: Why are you blaming SoulGlad for everything? What does not getting into university have to do with a drink anyway?

t_ch: 那些宇宙中的补品饮料都炒到几十万信用点了，我们苏乐达只需要几张苜蓿币就能带走，你还不满足？
t_en: Those supplements and health tonics get sold for tens of thousands of credits elsewhere in the universe, but you can walk off with a bottle of SoulGlad for just a few Alfalfa credits! What is there to gripe about?

t_ch: 我都懒得跟你说，怕这瓶盖被气得飞出来打你脸——再多骂点！
t_en: I don't even want to keep talking to you anymore in case my cap bursts off with rage and hits you in the face... Insult me more!

t_ch: 你发型不错。
t_en: I like your haircut.

t_ch: 那当然，这可是全苏乐达界最潮的一款瓶盖。
t_en: Of course, this is the most fashionable bottle cap style in all of the SoulGlad world.

t_ch: 不是——{F#姐}{M#哥}，是让你骂我，不是夸我！
t_en: No... {F#Lady}{M#Bro}, I said insult me, not compliment me!

t_ch: 我体内的气泡还远远不够，赶快再骂两句！
t_en: I've still got nowhere near enough bubbles in me! Hit me with another insult, quickly!

t_ch: 我喜欢孜然味苏乐达。
t_en: I like cumin-flavored SoulGlad.

t_ch: 他气泡的！那是什么邪道饮料，宇宙怎么会容许如此亵渎的苏乐达存在？
t_en: What the BUBBLE!? What kind of cursed drink is that? How could the universe allow such a blasphemous SoulGlad to exist?

t_ch: 我要向博识学会举报，居然有疯狂科学家做出这种违背苏乐达伦理的口味。
t_en: I'm going to report this to the Intelligentsia Guild. I cannot believe that some mad scientist would defy the ethics of SoulGlad like this!

t_ch: 看看这些快让我炸开的气泡，我承认你编的故事让我愤怒了——你是编的吧？
t_en: Look at all these bubbles. I'm about to explode! I admit that this story you made up has angered me... You did make it up, right?

t_ch: 抱歉，做不到。
t_en: Sorry, I can't do it.

t_ch: 我真骂不下去，太尴尬了。
t_en: I can't insult you. It's too awkward.

t_ch: 哎，这大概就是我的命吧。
t_en: Ah, I guess this is just my fate...

t_ch: 让我看看时间——糟糕，要开场了！顾不上气泡够不够了，在台上失败总比来不及上场强。
t_en: Let me check the time... Oh no, the competition is starting! I've got no time to worry about whether I'm bubbly enough... Failing on stage is better than missing the competition!

t_ch: 再见，好心人，我在苏乐达地窖的颁奖台等你！
t_en: Farewell, kind soul. I shall await you at the podium in the SoulGlad cellar!

t_ch: 在你的注视下，这瓶上进苏乐达以一种诡异的姿态飞走了，希望它能如愿以偿吧。
t_en: Under your watchful gaze, the SoulGlad bottle flies off in a bizarre stance. Let's hope they get what they wish for.

t_ch: 啊…是你啊，我真没脸见你……
t_en: Ah... It's you. I can't bring myself to face you.

t_ch: 我和对手的差距太大了，那点愤怒补充的气泡根本不够。
t_en: The gap between my competition and me was far too big. I did not work up anywhere near enough rage bubbles.

t_ch: 果然，对于我们这种出身平凡的苏乐达来说，想要成为这个地窖的一员，还是太异想天开了。
t_en: I guess I was right. It's just a wild fantasy for a SoulGlad of ordinary origins like myself to become a member of this cellar.

t_ch: 别说是只存在于都市传说里的秘密窖藏，就连展柜里的那些前辈，也远非我所能企及。
t_en: I can't even hope to match my seniors in the display cabinet, let alone the secret cellar stash of urban legends!

t_ch: 唉，我已经不会再愤怒了，以后就老实做瓶没气的糖浆饮料，等着被倒进马桶吧。
t_en: Ah... I have no more rage left in me. From now on I will just live my life as a bottle of flat syrup and wait to be poured down the toilet.

t_ch: 我已经不会再愤怒了，以后就老实做瓶没气的糖浆饮料吧……
t_en: I have no more rage left in me. From now on I will just live my life as a bottle of flat syrup...

t_ch: 你来啦，骂人宗师，一起来庆祝巨星苏乐达的诞生吧！
t_en: You're here, master of insults! Let us celebrate the birth of a new SoulGlad Superstar together!

t_ch: 你是没看到，当时我把瓶盖一拧开，愤怒的气泡直接像燃料一样把我推上颁奖台，整个赛场都凝固了，一半的参赛苏乐达直接当场弃权了。
t_en: You weren't there to see it, but the moment I opened my cap, my bubbles of rage shot me onto the podium like rocket fuel! The entire stadium froze and half of the contestants dropped out on the spot!

t_ch: 哼，所谓的苏乐达地窖，也不过如此。
t_en: Hah, is this all the legendary "SoulGlad cellar" has to offer?

t_ch: 依我看，别说是展柜里那些跟不上时代的老东西，就连只存在于都市传说中的秘密窖藏，恐怕也早已被我超越了。
t_en: The way I see it, it's not just those outdated old folks in the display cabinet, but I've already far surpassed the level of even the secret cellar stash of urban legends!

t_ch: 趁我们还能聊天，让你沾沾喜气。等我进到那边的展柜，以后你就只有瞻仰的份了！
t_en: I shall allow you to bathe in my success while we are still able to talk. Once I enter that display cabinet, you will only be able to look up at me in admiration!

t_ch: 趁我们还能聊天，让你沾沾喜气。等我进到那边的展柜，以后你就只有瞻仰的份了！
t_en: I shall allow you to bathe in my success while we are still able to talk. Once I enter that display cabinet, you will only be able to look up at me in admiration!

t_ch: 比赛马上就要…
t_en: The competition is about to...

t_ch: 完了完了完了。
t_en: Oh no oh no oh no.

t_ch: 这是一本手札。纸张边角已经泛黄，绝大部分手记已被红锈似的渍印污染，无从辨明。
t_en: A notebook — its corners are yellowed and the majority of the notes within have been rendered incomprehensible by some kind of red rust-like stains.

t_ch: 手札的内页隐隐留有疑似「知更鸟」字样的痕迹——或许与那位优雅的歌者小姐有关。
t_en: The journal's pages bear traces of what appears to be words resembling "Robin" — it could be related to that elegant songstress.

t_ch: （先收起来好了……）
t_en: (I'll keep this safe for now...)

t_ch: 嘿，{NICKNAME}，这边！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}, over here!

t_ch: 我们在哪里见过么？
t_en: Have we met somewhere before?

t_ch: 知更鸟？是你吗？
t_en: Robin? Is that you?

t_ch: 是我，知更鸟。这副面孔说话不太方便，稍等……
t_en: It's me. It's a bother talking with this face, hang on...

t_ch: 应该没人注意到这边，我们聊聊吧。怎么样，有什么发现吗？
t_en: I don't think anyone noticed us. Let's talk. Did you find anything?

t_ch: 虽然不易察觉，但你确实注意到她一时间似乎怔住了。但她很快恢复镇静，对你笑了笑——尽管声音依旧有些颤抖。
t_en: The reaction is almost imperceptible, but you definitely notice her momentary shock. She restores her composure very quickly, and flashes a smile at you — even if her voice trembles ever so slightly.

t_ch: …谢谢你，让我看看吧。
t_en: ...Thank you. I will have a look.

t_ch: 你的脸色看起来不太好。
t_en: You don't look so good.

t_ch: 我…抱歉。我没事，谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I... Sorry, I'm fine. Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这是我唯一能找到的线索了。
t_en: This is the only clue I found.

t_ch: 已经…足够了。谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It is... enough. Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这…确实是哥哥的笔迹，应该是他的东西没错。可这经文…《谐乐颂》里没有类似的记载。
t_en: This is... definitely my big brother's handwriting, it should be one of his belongings. But this scripture... The Odes of Harmony has nothing like this recorded in it.

t_ch: 还有…这些红色的污渍。他果然……
t_en: And... this red residue. So he really...

t_ch: 知更鸟哽住了。
t_en: Robin chokes on her words.

t_ch: 我很遗憾。
t_en: I'm really sorry.

t_ch: 知更鸟只兀自低头，怔怔地看着怀里的笔记本，什么也没有说。半晌，她终于开口。
t_en: Robin simply bows her head, staring dumbly at the journal in her hands, and says nothing. She finally speaks after a long silence.

t_ch: 事情也许还有转机。
t_en: We might still be able to turn this thing around.

t_ch: 知更鸟只兀自低头，怔怔地看着怀里的笔记本，什么也没有说。半晌，她终于开口。
t_en: Robin simply bows her head, staring dumbly at the journal in her hands, and says nothing. She finally speaks after a long silence.

t_ch: 嗯，一定会的。
t_en: Yes, we will.

t_ch: 从我们在流梦礁重逢时起，我就隐隐有种预感。也许，我们的道路再也不会有交集了……
t_en: When we reunited at Dreamflux Reef, I had an ominous feeling. That maybe, our paths would not cross again...

t_ch: 只是…没想到这一天来得这么快呢。
t_en: It's just... I didn't expect this day to come so soon.

t_ch: 真是天意弄人啊。
t_en: Heaven's will certainly is cruel.

t_ch: 「天意弄人」…呵呵，谁说不是呢。
t_en: "Heaven's will"... Heh-heh, who said it wasn't.

t_ch: 你今后有什么打算？
t_en: What do you intend to do?

t_ch: 我…不知道。千头万绪，一时间实在是让人无所适从。
t_en: I... don't know. There are too many things happening, I'm too confused to process them right now.

t_ch: 明明我们每个人——你、我、我的哥哥、我们所有人——我们都怀抱善意，想要把每一件事做到最好，并为此拼尽全力…可这无数善意最终却只酿成了悲剧。
t_en: All of us — you, me, my brother, all of us — we obviously have nothing but kind intentions, to do the best we can in all we do, and to give it our all... but our countless kind acts only end up in tragedy.

t_ch: 这个世界…还真是一点都不美好啊。
t_en: This world... is really not very beautiful at all.

t_ch: 但我们可以让它变得更好。
t_en: But we can make it better.

t_ch: 你说的没错——我们还有许多能做的事。
t_en: You're right. There's plenty more we can do.

t_ch: 我们不可能总是如愿以偿。
t_en: We can't always get what we want.

t_ch: 你说的没错，不过…我们还有许多能做的事。
t_en: You're right, but... we still have much more to do.

t_ch: 美梦如今摇摇欲坠，许多不怀好意的人也闻风而动。眼下，我无论如何都得保护好这里，然后排除万难，把它建设得更好…哥哥也一定希望我这么做。
t_en: The dream is on the verge of collapse, and many sinister characters are raring to capitalize on the situation. I have to protect this place at all costs, cull it of all impurities, then turn it into something better... I'm sure Sunday would also want me to do so.

t_ch: 说到哥哥——虽然我们只找到了这本手记，但我觉得这并不能说明什么。直觉告诉我，哥哥现在肯定还好好的，就在美梦的某处…正在为我们共同的理想寻找新的出路。
t_en: Speaking of my brother... though we only found this journal, I don't think it means anything right now. My instincts tell me that he's definitely alive and well somewhere in this dream... and looking for a new way out for the ideal that we share.

t_ch: 毕竟…天空才是鸟儿的归宿呀。
t_en: I mean... the sky is where the birds belong.

t_ch: 愿你们的理想早日实现。
t_en: May your ideals come true soon.

t_ch: 嗯，承你吉言。我一定不会退缩的——一如既往。
t_en: Yes, I will gladly take your blessing. I'll never back down — nor have I ever.

t_ch: 无论结果如何，愿你不留遗憾。
t_en: No matter what, I hope you have no regrets.

t_ch: 嗯，但愿如此。不过…我一定不会退缩的——一如既往。
t_en: Yes, I hope so too. I'll never back down — nor have I ever.

t_ch: 在那之前，你得保护好自己。
t_en: But first, you have to protect yourself.

t_ch: 嗯，我明白。不过…我一定不会退缩的——一如既往。
t_en: Yes, I understand. But... I'll never back down — nor have I ever.

t_ch: 就先到这里吧，谢谢你陪我聊了这么久…估计那帮猎犬已经发觉我又偷偷溜出来了吧。
t_en: Let's leave it here for now. Thank you for chatting with me for so long... I'm guessing the Hounds have already discovered that I've slipped out.

t_ch: 不好意思，{NICKNAME}，我必须得回去啦。也愿「同谐」的光芒常伴你……
t_en: I'm sorry, {NICKNAME}, but I have to return now. May the Harmony's light be with you...

t_ch: 话音未落，一张略微褪色的卡片便从笔记本的夹层中跌落。
t_en: Just before the last words drop, a faded card falls out from between the journal's folds.

t_ch: 咦？这是……
t_en: Huh? This is...

t_ch: 这是什么？
t_en: Huh. What's this?

t_ch: 这是…「盛会之星」系列收藏卡牌，不过是限量版。{RUBY_B#梦星娱乐}发行商{RUBY_E#}当年赠给我以示纪念，但我想它或许对哥哥更有纪念意义，就写上祝福语转赠给他了……
t_en: This is... a Planet of Festivities collectible card, but it's a limited edition. The {RUBY_B#Dreamstar Entertainment}distributor{RUBY_E#} gave it to me as a commemorative souvenir, but I thought it would mean more to my brother, so I wrote some kind words on it and gave it to him...

t_ch: 我也有张类似的卡片…
t_en: I also have a card that resembles this...

t_ch: 不，这张是…限量版。{RUBY_B#梦星娱乐}发行商{RUBY_E#}当年赠给我以示纪念，但我想它或许对哥哥更有纪念意义，就写上祝福语转赠给他了……
t_en: No, this is a... limited edition. The {RUBY_B#Dreamstar Entertainment}distributor{RUBY_E#} gave it to me as a commemorative souvenir, but I thought it would mean more to my brother, so I wrote some kind words on it and gave it to him...

t_ch: 呵呵，真没想到…他居然还留着这个……
t_en: Heh-heh, I never thought... he'd still have this...

t_ch: 都这么多年了……
t_en: It's been so many years...

t_ch: 他…一定过得很孤独吧……
t_en: He... must be really lonely...

t_ch: 她神情赧然，以一个不甚完美的微笑向你致歉，便低头掩面匆匆离去。
t_en: Her face flushes red, and with a less-than-perfect smile, she excuses herself and leaves in a fluster with a bowed head.

t_ch: 当你翻开略微有些滞涩的枯页，一丛光彩流溢的气泡兀自腾起。它们在空中滞留不多时便化作泡影——霎那间，朦胧的声音自你耳边响起……
t_en: When you flip that rather withered, brittle page, a burst of bubbles brimming with bright colors rises from it. They do not linger long in the air and shifts into mirages — at that moment, you hear a faint sound next to your ears...

t_ch: 胜者有胜者的义务。了结我…然后飞向天空吧。
t_en: The victor bears the responsibility of victory. Finish me... and fly into the sky.

t_ch: 我们…本该一起飞向天空的。
t_en: We were supposed... to fly into the sky together.

t_ch: 我也好想…和你一起啊。
t_en: If only... I could...
(What's this...)

t_ch: 当你集中注意力，想要追索声音的来源时，却发现它们已于自帷幕中落下的天光中如晨雾般消散，杳无踪迹。
t_en: When you focus and try to track down the source of the sound, you only discover that they have disappeared like morning dew in the light that pours down from the curtains, fading without a trace.

t_ch: 哎，我当初就不该犹豫的。
t_en: *tut* I should never have hesitated in the first place.

t_ch: 点开页面的时候还显示「有货」，结果…选特典的时候多看了几眼，回过神来就售罄了，那些抢到《空气蛹》专辑的人都是什么速度啊。
t_en: When I opened the page, it still said "In Stock" — but... I lingered too long on the options when I was getting the deluxe edition, but by the time I went back to the order, it had already been sold out. The fans who managed to get the INSIDE album sure are fast.

t_ch: 家人，我理解你的心情。我是验证码输慢了点，后面就再也登不进去了，怎么刷新都没反应。
t_en: My man, I know just how you feel. I was just too slow with my verification code, and I couldn't log into the site again no matter how many times I refreshed the page.

t_ch: 明明都成功下单了，智械还不用识别人脸，却倒在了最后这步，我恨啊！
t_en: The order clearly went through and Intellitrons don't even need the facial recognition process, but I was defeated at the last step. I hate it!

t_ch: 你们在聊什么呢？
t_en: What are you folks chatting about?

t_ch: 我说，你不会还不知道吧？能在梦境里引起这么大轰动的，当然只有一件事——知更鸟小姐的新专辑《空气蛹》终于发售了！
t_en: Surely you know, right? There's only one thing that could ever cause a big ruckus like this in the dreamscape, and that's Miss Robin finally releasing her new album, INSIDE!

t_ch: 围在这里的人群，都是知更鸟小姐的粉丝。哼，我没抢到新专辑，怎么想都是这群人的错。
t_en: All these people here are fans of Miss Robin. Hmph, seeing as I couldn't get my hands on the new album, it must be all these people's fault.

t_ch: 专辑很难买到吗？
t_en: Is the album really hard to buy?

t_ch: 岂止是困难啊，简直比成功偷渡进匹诺康尼的概率还要低！
t_en: Hard to buy? Why, you'd probably have a better chance of smuggling yourself into Penacony!

t_ch: 不到半秒钟，所有商品库存都被抢光了。没办法，谁让全宇宙到处都是知更鸟小姐的歌迷呢，大家都在等这个时候。
t_en: All of the stock was sold out in less than half a second. It's just the way it is... Miss Robin has fans everywhere throughout the universe, and they've all been holding out for just this moment.

t_ch: 辛苦攒下来的钱，到头来却花不出去。就像我对知更鸟小姐的爱明明是如此热烈，却找不到释放的出口，心里实在憋得慌啊。
t_en: Scrimping and saving all that hard-earned cash, only for me to have nowhere to spend it. It's just like this burning passion I have for Miss Robin — there's nowhere for me to release any of it. I can't keep it bottled up inside.

t_ch: 哎。
t_en: Ah...

t_ch: 哎。
t_en: Ah...

t_ch: 还有其他的购买渠道吗？
t_en: Aren't there any other ways you can buy it?

t_ch: 你也是知更鸟小姐的粉丝？想买的话，当然能找到渠道，只是……
t_en: Are you a fan of Miss Robin too? If you want it, you can always find a way. It's just...

t_ch: 总有人要把手里囤的货出掉，但我不建议你找他们购买。想买这张专辑的粉丝很多，价格也自然水涨船高，另一方面……
t_en: You'll always find someone who's stockpiled the albums and can sell you one, but I don't recommend buying from them. So many fans want to get their hands on it that of course the price has been driven up. And also...

t_ch: <size=28>我就是不愿意看那些倒卖贩子赚到钱！</size>
t_en: <size=28>I don't want to see any of those scalpers earn a single cent!</size>

t_ch: 还未来得及发表看法，你忽然感觉袖子被人轻轻扯了下。
t_en: Before you even get the chance to speak your mind, you suddenly feel a gentle tugging at your sleeve.

t_ch: …朋友，刚到的货，知更鸟的新专辑《空气蛹》，要看看吗？
t_en: Psst, hey buddy, I just got these goods. It's Miss Robin's new INSIDE album. Wanna check it out?

t_ch: 正品保证，全新无划痕，非二次包装，价钱绝对公道。数量有限，先到先得！
t_en: Guaranteed legit, brand-new condition, in original packaging, and at an absolutely fair price. My stocks are limited — first come first served!

t_ch: 你就是倒卖专辑的人啊！
t_en: So you're the one who's been scalping records!

t_ch: 你不知道这是违法的吗？
t_en: Haven't you heard that's illegal?

t_ch: 嗨，这么认真干嘛，都是为了挣信用点。再说，要不是有我帮忙，你们还抢不到这张专辑呢。收不收？没兴趣我就去问其他人了。
t_en: Hey, why so serious? I'm just earning a few credits here. Besides, you'd never get a copy of the album without my help. Do you want it, or not? Otherwise I can always go ask someone else.

t_ch: 我们不会买。
t_en: We're not interested.

t_ch: 要价多少？可以刀吗？
t_en: How much? Can you cut us a deal?

t_ch: 都好说！相遇就是缘，互相帮助是应该的。所以你看…原价的五倍怎么样？
t_en: No biggie! Karma brought us together. It's only right that we scratch each other's back. So then... what do you say — how about five times the sticker price?

t_ch: 会不会太贵了？
t_en: Isn't that a little steep?

t_ch: 想想，这才是你深切爱着知更鸟小姐的证明啊。你不收，有的是粉丝愿意收。
t_en: Think about it — this is how you can demonstrate your love for Miss Robin. If you don't want it, there are plenty of other fans who do.

t_ch: 我要走咯？
t_en: Should I be off, then?

t_ch: ——等等。我买了！
t_en: ...Hang on. I'll take it!

t_ch: 好嘞。信用点稍后打到账户里就好。感谢惠顾！这位客人还在思考吗？
t_en: Nice. Pop the credits into my account soon and that'll be that. Thanks for the business! You there — still considering?

t_ch: 我、我也…买一张…好了。
t_en: I—I'll... I'll also... take one... okay?

t_ch: 感谢惠顾！两位真是知更鸟小姐最忠实的粉丝啊，我深表尊敬。这位灰色的朋友怎么想呢？再不出手，货就要卖完咯？
t_en: Thanks for the business! You two truly are the most devoted fans of Miss Robin, and I sincerely take my hat off to you. What do you say then, my gray buddy? If you don't act soon, it'll be sold out.

t_ch: 我要向猎犬家系举报你。
t_en: I'm going to snitch you out to the Bloodhound Family.

t_ch: 啧，还真是冥顽不灵。罢了罢了，我再去找其他人。
t_en: Tsk, how pigheaded. That's fine. I'll just find someone else.

t_ch: …要价多少？可以刀吗？
t_en: ...How much? Can you cut us a deal?

t_ch: 尽管将声音压到了最低，但做出这种违反公序良俗的事，你仍然感受到了内心的煎熬。为此，你必须提交一个「崇高道德的赞许」才能解脱。
t_en: Despite keeping your voice to a whisper, you can't help but feel tormented at doing something that violates public order like this. To free you of this feeling, you'll have to submit a Praise of High Morals.

t_ch: 没问题，我就是想买。
t_en: No problem. I'm keen to buy it.

t_ch: 感谢惠顾！我现在就把货交给各位，街道上人多，请随我来这边。
t_en: Thanks for the business! Let me dish out the wares to everyone then. It's crowded around here, so please follow me.

t_ch: 那我还是选择举报吧。
t_en: Then I guess I'll just snitch you out, then.

t_ch: 啧，还真是冥顽不灵。罢了罢了，我再去找其他人。
t_en: Tsk, how pigheaded. That's fine. I'll just find someone else.

t_ch: …到这里就行，各位可以准备付款了，我把货拿出来给你们验验。
t_en: ...This is enough, please. Get your payments ready, everyone. I'll get the wares for you to inspect.

t_ch: 没想到…最后还是从倒卖贩子手里高价买到的。
t_en: I never thought I'd end up paying an arm and a leg to a scalper for it.
